titleStmt.title: SII 27.173: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 173. (A.R. No. 76 of 1943-44.) RĀMGAḌH, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab leaning against the front wall of Rāmadēva temple.

This record, engraved in slightly cursive characters, states Gōpāyi, her younger brother Rāmaṇa and others had the temple of Rāmanāthadēva construsted at Hosamaleyadurga, in honour of Rāmanāthavoḍeya of Hosamale, son of Khaṇḍeyarāya-Kaṁpilarāya and Vīra-Gujala-Hariharadēvī and grand-son of Mummaḍi Siṅga, and several others who fell in battle.
It is dated Śaka 1450, Sarvajit, Āśvija śu. 10, the details being insufficient for verification. The tithi corresponds to 1527 A.D., October 4, when the weekday was Friday. The Śaka year was current.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī gaṇādhipati śrī namaḥ . śubhamastu
śālivāhana śakhavaruṣa 1450 neya sarva
jitu saṁvatsarada Āśvijaśu10lū śrī kriṣṇa
dēvamahārāyaru pṛthvīrājyaṁ pālisu
ttirilū hosamaleya durgadalū muta
muṁmaḍi siṁgana kumāra khaṁḍeyarāya
kaṁpilarāyana paṭakesaluva rāṇīvāsa vīra gujala hari
haradēviyavara putrahosamaleya rāmanā
tha voḍeyaru chappanadēśada rāyaragaṁḍa Ātana
maneya hāḍukāṟa paṭada sōmaṇayya
vijayanagarada hūvina bāgila hanumaṁta
dēvara pūjāri rāmaṇanu Ātanamaganu sōmaṇa
yana śiṣya rāmanāthana kōnērināyakana magaḷu kā
ṭi nāyaka voḍeya haḷada rāmanāthana gōpāyi
Ākeyataṁma rāmanātha dēvara toḷada rāmaṇanu
hosamaleya durgada rāmanāthadēvara guḍiyanu
kaṭistaru gaṁṭe jēgaṭe harivāṇa jeṁbuyi
nu samarpisi rāmanāthana kālada pūrvada chappanna
maṁdi vīrarige vīrasvargga Āgabēkeṁdu kaṭistaru
chappana dēśada
hāḍukāṟa
ṟoḍe
damaged
ṟacuva hāḍukāṟa bēgemastakāśūla gōpaLines 19 to 24 are engraved on the left margin of an arched space below the main record and line 25 on the right margin of it.
yivaru samarppisida pādatīrthaLine 26
Digital edition of SII 27.173 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
